data.title: Frontier Management Revamps Board Lineup With Two New Director Candidates
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Frontier Management, Inc. ( (JP:7038) ) has shared an update.

Frontier Management Inc. has nominated four candidates for director positions, including two new directors and two incumbents, to be voted on at its 19th Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders on March 26, 2026. The move is aimed at revitalizing the management structure as the current terms of several directors end.

Deputy President and Executive Officer Sumio Nishizawa and Managing Executive Officer Kyogo Murase have been nominated as new directors, reflecting their track records in driving sales, winning large projects, and leading company-wide business reforms. Their elevation to the board underscores Frontier Management’s focus on strengthening governance and execution capabilities to support medium- to long-term growth and enhance corporate value for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:7038) stock is a Sell with a Yen534.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Frontier Management, Inc. stock, see the JP:7038 Stock Forecast page.

More about Frontier Management, Inc.

Frontier Management Inc., listed on the TSE Prime Market, operates in the professional services and consulting industry. The company focuses on strategy and operations consulting, corporate development support, and advisory services for financial institutions and other corporate clients across Japan, leveraging a network of regional branches and specialized divisions.

Average Trading Volume: 66,661

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: Yen6.63B

data.title: FirstCape Group Builds 5% Substantial Stake in Spark New Zealand
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spark New Zealand Limited ( (AU:SPK) ) has issued an announcement.

Spark New Zealand has disclosed that FirstCape Group Limited has become a substantial product holder in the company, accumulating a 5.06% stake comprising 95,643,717 ordinary shares. The position is held through several wholly owned subsidiaries, including Harbour Asset Management, BNZ Investment Services, and JBWere (NZ), which collectively exercise voting and investment management powers on behalf of underlying clients.

The new substantial holding underscores continued institutional interest in Spark’s equity and further concentrates ownership among professional asset managers. This development may enhance liquidity in Spark’s shares and signals ongoing confidence from large investors in the company’s long-term prospects within New Zealand’s telecommunications sector.

More about Spark New Zealand Limited

Spark New Zealand Limited is a major telecommunications and digital services provider in New Zealand, offering mobile, fixed-line, broadband, and data services to consumers, businesses, and public sector clients. The company operates in a highly competitive market, where institutional investors and asset managers play a significant role in its ownership structure and market valuation.

Average Trading Volume: 1,058,870

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$3.42B

Atreides Management, LP, managed by Gavin Baker, recently executed a significant transaction involving Microsoft ((MSFT)). The hedge fund increased its position by 181,468 shares.

Recent Updates on Microsoft stock

Microsoft shares have been volatile, recently sliding as much as 18% over a month before partially rebounding, with the stock now roughly flat to modestly higher over 12 months versus recent closes around $393–$482. Despite the pullback, Wall Street maintains a StrongBuy consensus and average 12‑month targets near $600+, implying substantial upside.

Most analysts highlight strong cloud and AI momentum, with Azure growing about 37–39% and commercial RPO surging to roughly $625 billion, supported by major OpenAI and Anthropic commitments and rapid Copilot adoption. A notable dissent comes from Stifel’s Brad Reback, who downgraded MSFT to Hold on concerns that 2027 revenue and EPS expectations, Azure acceleration, and capex—potentially reaching ~$200 billion in FY27—may cap near‑term upside and keep shares range‑bound.

data.title: Revolver Resources Cancels Director Performance Rights After Hurdle Missed
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Revolver Resources Holdings Ltd ( (AU:RRR) ) has provided an announcement.

Revolver Resources has disclosed a change in director Patrick Williams’ interests following the cancellation of a portion of his Class C performance rights. The company reported that 3,431,666 Class C performance rights held indirectly through an associated entity were cancelled after the specified performance hurdle was not met.

Following the cancellation, Williams’ indirect holdings continue to include a substantial stake in fully paid ordinary shares, multiple tranches of unlisted options, and a reduced balance of Class C performance rights. The adjustment reflects the company’s adherence to performance-based incentive structures for executives and directors, aligning equity rewards with the achievement of predefined milestones.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RRR) stock is a Hold with a A$0.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Revolver Resources Holdings Ltd stock, see the AU:RRR Stock Forecast page.

More about Revolver Resources Holdings Ltd

Revolver Resources Holdings Ltd is an Australian-listed resources company focused on mineral exploration and development. The company’s activities center on acquiring and advancing resource projects, with securities including fully paid ordinary shares, unlisted options, and performance rights held by directors and related entities.

data.title: EDU Holdings Chair Gary Burg Steps Down, Board Names Interim Successor
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EDU Holdings Limited ( (AU:EDU) ) has issued an update.

EDU Holdings has announced the immediate resignation of Non-Executive Chair and Director Gary Burg due to health reasons, ending a decade-long tenure that began after the company’s 2016 recapitalisation and ASX relisting. Burg, who led the supporting investor consortium, departs at what he describes as a positive stage in EDU’s evolution and is credited by the board with providing steady guidance through both challenging and defining periods.

Long-standing Non-Executive Director Peter Mobbs has been appointed Interim Non-Executive Chair to ensure leadership continuity while the board begins a formal search for a permanent successor. The transition marks a significant governance change for EDU but is framed as orderly and respectful, aiming to minimise disruption to operations and reassure stakeholders of the company’s stability and ongoing strategic direction.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:EDU) stock is a Hold with a A$0.79 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on EDU Holdings Limited stock, see the AU:EDU Stock Forecast page.

More about EDU Holdings Limited

EDU Holdings Limited is an Australian education company listed on the ASX, providing education services under the banner “Education With Purpose.” The group focuses on delivering vocational and higher education programs to domestic and international students from its base in Haymarket, New South Wales, positioning itself within the post-secondary education and training sector.

Average Trading Volume: 966,553

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$91.93M
